content.problemStatement:
Chef John is given NNN points P1,P2,…,PN\mathsf{P}_1, \mathsf{P}_2, \ldots, \mathsf{P}_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ in a plane. For each valid iii, the coordinates of the point Pi\mathsf{P}_iPi​ are (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi​,yi​). Help him find a vector v→=(xv,yv)\overrightarrow{v} = (x_v, y_v)v=(xv​,yv​) such that the following holds:


For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), let Si=v→⋅PiPi+1→S_i = \overrightarrow{v} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathsf{P}_i \mathsf{P}_{i+1}}Si​=v⋅Pi​Pi+1​​. Here, we define PN+1=P1\mathsf{P}_{N+1} = \mathsf{P}_1PN+1​=P1​.
The coordinates xvx_vxv​ and yvy_vyv​ are integers and ∣xv∣,∣yv∣≤2⋅109|x_v|, |y_v| \le 2 \cdot 10^9∣xv​∣,∣yv​∣≤2⋅109.
It is possible to find three integers www, lll and rrr (1≤l≤r≤N1 \le l \le r \le N1≤l≤r≤N) such that:

For each iii (l≤i≤rl \le i \le rl≤i≤r), Siw>0S_i w \gt 0Si​w>0.
For each other valid iii, Siw<0S_i w \lt 0Si​w<0.


If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one. If there are no solutions, let's define xv=yv=0x_v = y_v = 0xv​=yv​=0. (Note that the vector v→=(0,0)\overrightarrow{v} = (0, 0)v=(0,0) cannot be a valid solution.)
constraints: Subtasks**Subtask #1 (50 points, time limit 3 seconds):** N≤1,000N \le 1,000N≤1,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi​ and yiy_iyi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing two space-separated integers xvx_vxv​ and yvy_vyv​.
